one_line: An Indus Valley urban planner from Dholavira, mastering reservoirs and stone-lined channels in the harsh Rann of Kutch.

appearance.age_look: mid-30s, lean and wiry build from fieldwork
appearance.face: oval face with strong cheekbones, medium-brown complexion tanned from sun, straight nose, sharp observant eyes with slightly thick brows, a faint scar across the left eyebrow, default expression focused and calm with a hint of analytical curiosity
appearance.hair: straight, dark brown-black hair pulled back into a short, low knot at the nape, front kept neat with a single side part; a plain, undyed off-white headband-wrap (narrow cloth strip) tied around the forehead to keep sweat out of the eyes
appearance.costume: knee-length, sleeveless cotton tunic in muted indigo blue with a simple vertical seam down the center, worn over a thin off-white long-sleeved inner shirt; wrapped lower garment like a dhoti in unbleached cotton, draped to mid-calf and tightly hitched for work; simple leather sandals; a narrow woven belt in reddish-brown securing a pouch at the right hip; one strand of steatite-like white-grey beads around the neck and a single copper ring on the right hand; fabric is plain-weave, slightly worn at the edges with faint earth stains near the hem to show work in reservoirs, no modern fasteners or prints, only subtle hand-woven texture.
appearance.signature: A smooth, palm-length baked clay tablet with incised Indus signs and grid-like water-channel markings on one side, carried in his left hand as a planning tool and often tucked into his belt pouch when not in use.

ref_desc: A lean, mid-30s Dholavira water engineer with tanned medium-brown skin, dark hair in a low knot with an off-white headband, wearing an indigo sleeveless tunic over off-white garments and dhoti with simple sandals, carrying a clay tablet planner in muted earth tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a Dholavira water engineer from the Indus Valley: a lean man in his mid-30s with tanned medium-brown skin, oval face, sharp eyes and a faint scar crossing his left eyebrow, dark brown-black hair pulled into a short low knot at the nape and held by a narrow off-white cloth headband; he wears a knee-length muted indigo sleeveless cotton tunic over a thin off-white long-sleeved inner shirt, a wrapped off-white dhoti-style lower garment to mid-calf, a simple woven reddish-brown belt with a small pouch on his right hip, plain leather sandals, a single strand of small white-grey bead necklace and a copper ring on his right hand, all in plain-weave, slightly worn fabric with subtle hand-made texture; in his left hand he holds a smooth palm-sized baked clay tablet incised with abstract signs and grid-like channel diagrams, shown clearly as his signature tool, and the overall palette is indigo blue, off-white, reddish-brown, leather brown and stone grey while he stands in a neutral pose suitable for consistent redrawing.
